text.title: mutation-testing-ci-gate Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the mutation-testing-ci-gate methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each module has a documented kill-rate floor (per-language defaults: TS 60%, Python 55%, JVM 65%). Floors start lax and ratchet over 4-8 weeks.
rule.rationale: Static high floors break fleet velocity at adoption.

rule.statement: Mutation runs on diff (git diff --name-only origin/main) — never full repo on every PR.
Surviving mutants categorised as: real gap | equivalent-mutant | intentional. Each category has a documented triage policy.
rule.rationale: Untriaged equivalent mutants inflate the survivor count and erode trust in the score.

rule.statement: Mutators run only against code covered by tests. Mutating uncovered code produces noise.
rule.rationale: Mutants on dead code can never be killed — they aren't reached.

rule.statement: Mutation gate flips from continue-on-error to blocking only after >= 14 days of shadow telemetry with < 10% noise rate.
Per-PR `mutation-exception` label allows a single bypass with an audit log entry; repeated bypass triggers a review.
rule.rationale: Hard blockers without escape valves get bypassed silently.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
